title: Energy transformation device with reduced energy loses
abstract: Invention relates to an energy transformation device with reduced energy loses as well as on a method of operation thereof. The device and method for obtaining energy from gravity and permanent magnet offer a technical solution to obtain potential energy by means of weights (25; 26) and permanent magnets (23; 24) with reduced energy loss. In the 1st cycle, the weights (25; 26) and the permanent magnets (23; 24) lose potential energy gaining momentum. In the 2nd cycle, the weights (25; 26) and the permanent magnets (23; 24) receive a momentum from the flywheel (1), so that in the 3rd cycle, the weights (25; 26) and the permanent magnets (23; 24) could obtain potential energy with reduced momentum loss. In the 4th cycle, the weights (25; 26) and the permanent magnets (23; 24) return a momentum to the flywheel (1). The present invention may be used for battery charging, as well as provides environmentally friendly energy production.
claims:
Claims 1. Energy transformation device comprising: a casing (7); a first shaft (34) rotatably connected to the casing (7); a flywheel (1) fixed to the first shaft (34) and a first gear (31) fixed to the first shaft (34); a main shaft (9) rotatably connected to the casing (7); a first gear disc (29) fixed to the main shaft (9) and is geared with the first gear (31); a second shaft (4) rotatably connected to the casing (7); a first sprocket (5) fixed to the second shaft (4) and a second gear (3) fixed to the second shaft (4); a second gear disc (17) arranged to the main shaft (9) and is geared with the second gear (3); a second sprocket (16) fixed to the main shaft (9), wherein the first sprocket (5) is associated to the second sprocket (16) by means of a chain track (6); a main bevel gear (13) rotatably attached to the casing (7); a first pendulum (22) connected to the main shaft (9) so that the first pendulum (22) can rotate around the main shaft (9), wherein the first pendulum (22) comprises: a first weight (26) fixed thereto, a first permanent magnet (24) fixed to the first weight (26), a first bevel gear (12) fixed thereto but at the same time with ability to rotate around the main shaft (9); a first electromagnet (27) fixed thereto, a second pendulum (20) connected to the main shaft (9) so that the second pendulum (20) can rotate around the main shaft (9), wherein the second pendulum (20) comprises: a second weight (25) fixed thereto, a second permanent magnet (23) fixed to the second weight (25), a second bevel gear (8) fixed thereto but at the same time with ability to rotate around the main shaft (9), a second electromagnet (19) fixed thereto, wherein the main bevel gear (13) is geared to the first bevel gear (12) and the second bevel gear (8) so that the first pendulum (22) fixed to the first bevel gear (12) rotates in counter direction to the second pendulum (20) fixed to the second bevel gear (8); 

 wherein the device further comprises: a first disc (11) fixed to the casing (7) and arranged in proximity of the first bevel gear (12), wherein the first disc (11) comprises a first track (37); a second disc (10) fixed to the casing (7) and arranged in proximity of the second bevel gear (8), wherein the second disc (10) comprises a second track (38); a first pad (28) associated with the first electromagnet (27) so that upon activation of the first electromagnet (27) the first pad (28) is pressed against the first gear disc (29), but upon deactivation of the first electromagnet (27) the first pad (28) is retracted from the first gear disc (29); a second pad (18) associated with the second electromagnet (19) so that upon activation of the second electromagnet (19) the second pad (18) is pressed against the second gear disc (17), but upon deactivation of the second electromagnet (19) the second pad (18) is retracted from the second gear disc (17); a battery (21) electrically connected to the first track (37) and the second track (38); a first contact (14) fixed to the first pendulum (22) and configured to run on the first disc (11) and especially on the first track (37) of the first disc (11), wherein the first contact (14) is electrically connected to the first electromagnet (27) so that the first electromagnet (27) is activated when the first contact (14) runs across the first track (37) of the first disc (11); and a second contact (15) fixed to the second pendulum (20) and configured to run on the second disc (10) and especially on the second track (38) of the second disc (10), wherein the second contact (15) is electrically connected to the second electromagnet (19) so that the second electromagnet (19) is activated when the second contact (15) runs across the second track (38) of the second disc (10). 2. The device according to claim 1, characterized in that a mass of the first weight (26) and the first permanent magnet (24) is greater than a mass of the first pendulum (22), and a mass of the second weight (25) and the second permanent magnet (25) is greater than a mass of the second pendulum (20). 3. The device according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that the first permanent magnet (24) and the second permanent magnet (23) are closer to the casing (7) when the first weight (26) and the second weight (25) have lost 100% from its maximum potential 

 energy rather than when the first weight (26) and the second weight (25) have obtained 100% from its maximum potential energy. 4. The device according to any of preceding Claims, characterized in that the flywheel (1) is configured to provide a momentum to the first weight (26), the second weight (25), the first permanent magnet (24) and the second permanent magnet (23). 5. The device according to any of preceding Claims, characterized in that the first weight (26), the second weight (25), the first permanent magnet (24) and the second permanent magnet (23) are configured to rotate around the main shaft (9) so that the potential energy may be obtained and lost. 6. The device according to any of preceding Claims, characterized in that the second track (38) is arranged on a one side of the second disc (10) so that the second track (38) interacts with the second contact (15) only for a predetermined length from the entire circular length of the second disc (10), and the first track (37) is arranged on a one side of the first disc (11) so that the first track (37) interacts with the first contact (14) only for a predetermined length from the entire circular length of the first disc (11). 7. The device according to Claim 6, characterized in that the arrangement of the first track (37) on the first disc (11) and arrangement of the second track (38) on the second disc (10) is such that the contacts (14; 15) are brought into the contact with the tracks (37; 38) for predetermined circular length from the overall length of the discs (11; 10). 8. The device according to Claim 6 or 7, characterized in that the arrangement of the second track (38) on the second disc (10) is such that the second pad (18) is pressed against the second gear disc (17) from the moment when the first weight (26) and the second weight (25) have obtained about 90% from its maximal potential energy till the moment when the first weight (26) and the second weight (25) have lost about 10% from its maximal potential energy. 9. The device according to any of preceding Claims, characterized in that the weights (26; 25) and the permanent magnets (24; 23) are configured to increase angular velocity and to obtain momentum due to gravitational force while losing potential energy. 10. The device according to any of preceding Claims, characterized in that the first track (37) and the second track (38) are arranged on the first disc (11) and the second disc (10) respectively so that the first contact (14) and the second contact (15) are brought into the contact with the first track (37) and the second track (38) respectively, wherein the first contact (14) is brought into the contact with the first track (37) from the moment when the weights (25; 26) have lost 90% from their maximum potential energy until the weights (25; 26) have obtained 90% from their maximum potential energy, and wherein the second contact (15) is brought into the contact with the second track (38) from the moment when the weights (25; 26) have obtained 90% from their maximum potential energy until the weights (25; 26) have lost 10% from their maximum potential energy.. 11. The device according to any of preceding Claims, characterized in that the device is configured to provide the angular velocity of the first gear disc (29) higher than the angular velocity of the second gear disc (17). 12. A method of operating the device according to any of claims 1 to 11, wherein the operation of the device comprises the following four cycles: - in the first cycle, the weights (26; 25) by rotation around the main shaft (9) and due to gravitational force increase their angular velocity and obtain momentum losing their potential energy, wherein the permanent magnets (24; 23) by the same rotation around the main shaft (9) and due to gravitational force and magnetic force increase their angular velocity and obtain momentum losing their potential energy; - in the second cycle, the weights (26; 25) and the permanent magnets (24; 23) receive additional power from the flywheel ( 1 ), wherein the flywheel ( 1 ) is engaged when the weights (26; 25) have lost 90% from their maximum potential energy until the moment when the weights (26; 25) have obtained about 10% from their maximum potential energy; - in the third cycle, the weights (26; 25) and the permanent magnets (24; 23) obtain potential energy with a higher average angular velocity than the average angular velocity of the weights (26; 25) and the permanent magnets (24; 23) during the first cycle; and - in the fourth cycle, the weights (26; 25) and the permanent magnets (24; 23) return obtained momentum to the flywheel (1) via the second pad (18), the second gear 

 disc (17), the second gear (3), the second shaft (4), the first sprocket (5), the chain track (6), the second sprocket (16), the main shaft (9), the first gear disc (29) and the first gear (31). 13. The method according to claim 12, characterized in that in the first cycle, the first track (37) and the second track (38) are not into the contact with the first contact (14) and the second contact (15) from the moment when the weights (25; 26) have lost 10% of their maximum potential energy until the weights (25; 26) have lost 90% of their maximum potential energy; in the second cycle and the third cycle, the first contact (14) is brought into the contact with the first track (37) from the moment when the weights (25; 26) have lost 90% of their maximum potential energy until the weights (25; 26) have obtained 90% of their maximum potential energy; and in the fourth cycle, the second contact (15) is brought into the contact with the second track (38) from the moment when the weights (25; 26) have obtained 90% of their maximum potential energy until the weights (25; 26) have lost about 10% of their maximum potential energy.
